ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n6\t2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n7\t3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n8\t4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n9\t\n10\tThis utility is used across:\n11\t- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n12\t- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n13\t- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n14\t- Any other LLM interaction points\n15\t\"\"\"\n16\t\n17\timport json\n18\timport re\n19\tfrom logging import getLogger\n20\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n21\t\n22\t\n23\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n24\t\n25\t\n26\t# Precompile regex pattern for better performance (compiled once at module load)\n27\t_FENCE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^```(?:\\w+)?\\n([\\s\\S]+?)\\n```$\")\n28\t\n29\t\n30\tclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n31\t    \"\"\"Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\"\"\"\n32\t\n33\t    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n34\t        self.raw_response = raw_response\n35\t        super().__init__(message)\n36\t\n37\t\n38\tdef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n39\t    \"\"\"\n40\t    Strip markdown code fences from a response.\n41\t\n42\t    Handles:\n43\t    - ```json ... ```\n44\t    - ``` ... ```\n45\t    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n46\t\n47\t    Performance: Uses precompiled regex pattern and early returns to minimize\n48\t    string operations. Optimized for hot-path usage in LLM response parsing.\n49\t\n50\t    Args:\n51\t        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n52\t\n53\t    Returns:\n54\t        Text with markdown fences removed\n55\t\n56\t    Examples:\n57\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n58\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n59\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n60\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n61\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n62\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n63\t    \"\"\"\n64\t    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n65\t    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n66\t        return raw\n67\t\n68\t    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n69\t    text = raw.strip()\n70\t\n71\t    # Match code fence pattern: ```optional_lang content ```\n72\t    # Uses precompiled pattern for better performance\n73\t    match = _FENCE_PATTERN.match(text)\n74\t\n75\t    if match:\n76\t        # Only strip the matched content, not again\n77\t        return match.group(1)\n78\t\n79\t    return text\n80\t\n81\t\n82\tdef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: dict) -> dict:\n83\t    \"\"\"\n84\t    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n85\t\n86\t    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n87\t    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n88\t\n89\t    Args:\n90\t        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n91\t\n92\t    Returns:\n93\t        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n94\t\n95\t    Examples:\n96\t        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n97\t        {\"content\": [...]}\n98\t        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n99\t        {\"content\": [...]}\n100\t    \"\"\"\n101\t    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n102\t    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n103\t        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n104\t        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n105\t        return inner\n106\t\n107\t    return response_data\n108\t\n109\t\n110\tdef extract_text_from_response(payload: dict) -> str:\n111\t    \"\"\"\n112\t    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n113\t\n114\t    Args:\n115\t        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n116\t\n117\t    Returns:\n118\t        Extracted text content\n119\t\n120\t    Raises:\n121\t        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n122\t    \"\"\"\n123\t    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n124\t\n125\t    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n126\t        text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n127\t        if text:\n128\t            return text\n129\t\n130\t    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n131\t    logger.warning(f\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n132\t    return str(content)\n133\t\n134\t\n135\tdef parse_llm_response(\n136\t    raw_text: str,\n137\t    *,\n138\t    strip_fences: bool = True,\n139\t    expect_json: bool = True,\n140\t) -> Any:\n141\t    \"\"\"\n142\t    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n143\t\n144\t    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n145\t    It handles the common pattern of:\n146\t    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n147\t    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n148\t    3. Providing clear error messages\n149\t\n150\t    Performance: Optimized to minimize string operations and early-return on\n151\t    empty inputs. Fence stripping and JSON parsing happen in a single pass.\n152\t\n153\t    Args:\n154\t        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n155\t        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n156\t        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n157\t\n158\t    Returns:\n159\t        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n160\t\n161\t    Raises:\n162\t        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n163\t\n164\t    Examples:\n165\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n166\t        {'a': 1}\n167\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n168\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n169\t    \"\"\"\n170\t    # Early return for empty input\n171\t    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n172\t        if expect_json:\n173\t            raise ParseLLMError(\n174\t                \"Empty response provided\",\n175\t                raw_response=raw_text,\n176\t            )\n177\t        return raw_text\n178\t\n179\t    try:\n180\t        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n181\t        if strip_fences:\n182\t            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n183\t        else:\n184\t            text = raw_text.strip()\n185\t\n186\t        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n187\t        if expect_json:\n188\t            try:\n189\t                return json.loads(text)\n190\t            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n191\t                # Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n\nHandles:\n1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# Precompile regex pattern for better performance (compiled once at module load)\n_FENCE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^```(?:\\w+)?\\n([\\s\\S]+?)\\n```$\")\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses precompiled regex pattern and early returns to minimize\n    string operations. Optimized for hot-path usage in LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Match code fence pattern: ```optional_lang content ```\n    # Uses precompiled pattern for better performance\n    match = _FENCE_PATTERN.match(text)\n\n    if match:\n        # Only strip the matched content, not again\n        return match.group(1)\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: dict) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(f\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized to minimize string operations and early-return on\n    empty inputs. Fence stripping and JSON parsing happen in a single pass.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: dict,\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Map urgency string to enum\n            try:\n                urgency = Urgency(urgency_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                urgency = Urgency.NORMAL\n\n            # Build coverage and caveats from fetch result\n            coverage = None\n            caveats = None\n            if request.fetched_context:\n                coverage = {\n                    \"total_sources\": request.fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                    \"succeeded\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                    \"timed_out\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                    \"failed\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n                }\n                caveats = request.fetched_context.caveats\n\n            result = SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=urgency,\n                coverage=coverage,\n                caveats=caveats,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Synthesis complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(data)} data fields, urgency={urgency.value}\"\n            )\n\n            return result\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Pretty-print the data\n                    lines.append(f\"```json\")\n                    lines.append(json.dumps(source_result.data, indent=2))\n                    lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n53\t    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n54\t    pass\n55\t\n56\t\n57\tclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n58\t    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n59\t\n60\t    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n61\t        self.parse_error = parse_error\n62\t        self.raw_output = raw_output\n63\t        self.retry_count = retry_count\n64\t        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n65\t\n66\t\n67\tclass IntentType(Enum):\n68\t    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n69\t    STATUS = \"status\"\n70\t    ACTION = \"action\"\n71\t    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n72\t    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n73\t    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n74\t    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n75\t    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n76\t    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n77\t    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n78\t    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n79\t\n80\t\n81\t@dataclass\n82\tclass IntentClassification:\n83\t    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n84\t    intent_type: IntentType\n85\t    project_slug: str | None = None\n86\t    confidence: float = 1.0\n87\t    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n88\t    reasoning: str = \"\"\n89\t    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n90\t    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n91\t\n92\t\n93\t@dataclass\n94\tclass RoutedIntent:\n95\t    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n96\t    intent_id: str\n97\t    classification: IntentClassification\n98\t    session_id: str\n99\t    utterance: str\n100\t    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n101\t    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n102\t    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n103\t    router_ms: int | None = None\n104\t\n105\t\n106\t# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n107\t# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n108\t# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\n109\tROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n110\t\n111\t# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n112\t_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n113\t    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n114\t    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n115\t    \"intent objects.\"\n116\t)\n117\t\n118\t\n119\tclass IntentRouter:\n120\t    \"\"\"\n121\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n122\t\n123\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n124\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n125\t    \"\"\"\n126\t\n127\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n128\t        self.store = store\n129\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n130\t        self._zai_client = None\n131\t        self._reload_manager = None\n132\t\n133\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n134\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n135\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n136\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n137\t        return self._zai_client\n138\t\n139\t    async def _get_store(self):\n140\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n141\t        if self.store is None:\n142\t            self.store = get_store()\n143\t        return self.store\n144\t\n145\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n146\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n147\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n148\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n149\t        return self._reload_manager\n150\t\n151\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n152\t        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n153\t        try:\n154\t            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n155\t        except Exception as e:\n156\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n157\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n158\t\n159\t    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n160\t        \"\"\"\n161\t        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n162\t\n163\t        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n164\t        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n165\t        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n166\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n167\t        \"\"\"\n168\t        try:\n169\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n170\t        except Exception as e:\n171\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n172\t            return \"\"\n173\t\n174\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n175\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n176\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n177\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n178\t        if urgency_prompt:\n179\t            system_prompt = (\n180\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n181\t            )\n182\t        return system_prompt\n183\t\n184\t    async def classify_utterance(\n185\t        self,\n186\t        utterance: str,\n187\t        session_id: str,\n188\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n189\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n190\t        \"\"\"\n191\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n192\t\n193\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n194\t\n195\t        Args:\n196\t            utterance: The user utterance\n197\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n198\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n199\t\n200\t        Returns:\n201\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n202\t\n203\t        Raises:\n204\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n205\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n206\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n207\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n208\t        \"\"\"\n209\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n210\t\n211\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n212\t        store = await self._get_store()\n213\t        session_context = \"\"\n214\t        try:\n215\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n216\t            if session:\n217\t                # Get recent intents for context\n218\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n219\t                if recent_intents:\n220\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n221\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n222\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n223\t        except Exception as e:\n224\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n225\t\n226\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n227\t\n228\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n229\t\n230\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n231\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n232\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n233\t\n234\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n235\t        retry_count = 0\n236\t        raw_response = None\n237\t\n238\t        try:\n239\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n240\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n241\t                user_message=user_message,\n242\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n243\t                max_tokens=2048,\n244\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n245\t            )\n246\t\n247\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n248\t            raw_response = response\n249\t\n250\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n251\t            raw = response.strip()\n252\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n253\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n254\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n255\t\n256\t            # Parse JSON response\n257\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n258\t            classifications = []\n259\t\n260\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n261\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n262\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n263\t                try:\n264\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n265\t                except ValueError:\n266\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n267\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n268\t\n269\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n270\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n271\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n272\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n273\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n274\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n275\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n276\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n277\t                )\n278\t                classifications.append(classification)\n279\t\n280\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n281\t            return classifications\n282\t\n283\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n284\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n285\t\n286\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n287\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n288\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n289\t                retry_count += 1\n290\t                try:\n291\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n292\t                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n293\t                        utterance=utterance,\n294\t                        session_id=session_id,\n295\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n296\t                    )\n297\t                except json.JSONDecode as retry_e:\n298\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n299\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n300\t                        parse_error=str(e),\n301\t                        raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n302\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n303\t                    ) from retry_e\n304\t\n305\t            # No retry or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n306\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n307\t                parse_error=str(e),\n308\t                raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n309\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n310\t            ) from e\n311\t\n312\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n313\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n314\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n315\t\n316\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n317\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n318\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n319\t\n320\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n321\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n322\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n323\t\n324\t        except Exception as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n326\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n327\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n328\t\n329\t    async def route_utterance(\n330\t        self,\n331\t        utterance: str,\n332\t        utterance_id: str,\n333\t        session_id: str,\n334\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n335\t        \"\"\"\n336\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n337\t\n338\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n339\t\n340\t        Args:\n341\t            utterance: The user utterance\n342\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n343\t            session_id: Session ID\n344\t\n345\t        Returns:\n346\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n347\t\n348\t        Raises:\n349\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n350\t        \"\"\"\n351\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n352\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n353\t\n354\t        try:\n355\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n356\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n357\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n358\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n359\t            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n360\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n361\t\n362\t            routed_intents = []\n363\t            for classification in classifications:\n364\t                # Create intent ID\n365\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n366\t\n367\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n368\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n369\t                    classification=classification,\n370\t                    session_id=session_id,\n371\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n372\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n373\t                )\n374\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n375\t\n376\t            return routed_intents\n377\t\n378\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n379\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n380\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n381\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n382\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n383\t                utterance=utterance,\n384\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n385\t                session_id=session_id,\n386\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n387\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n388\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n389\t            )\n390\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n391\t\n392\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n393\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n394\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n395\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n396\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n397\t                utterance=utterance,\n398\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n399\t                session_id=session_id,\n400\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n401\t            )\n402\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n403\t\n404\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n405\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n406\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n407\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n408\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n409\t                utterance=utterance,\n410\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n411\t                session_id=session_id,\n412\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n413\t            )\n414\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n415\t\n416\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n417\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n418\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n419\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n420\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n421\t                utterance=utterance,\n422\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n423\t                session_id=session_id,\n424\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n425\t            )\n426\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n427\t\n428\t    async def process_intent(\n429\t        self,\n430\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n431\t    ) -> dict:\n432\t        \"\"\"\n433\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n434\t\n435\t        Args:\n436\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n437\t\n438\t        Returns:\n439\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n442\t\n443\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n444\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n445\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n446\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n447\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n448\t        # the dispatch itself.\n449\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n450\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n451\t\n452\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n453\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n454\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n455\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n456\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n457\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n458\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n459\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n460\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n461\t            )\n462\t\n463\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n464\t            store = await self._get_store()\n465\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n466\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n467\t                \"resolved\",\n468\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n469\t            )\n470\t\n471\t            return {\n472\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n473\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n474\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n475\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n476\t            }\n477\t\n478\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n479\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n480\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n481\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n482\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n483\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n484\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n485\t            )\n486\t\n487\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n488\t            store = await self._get_store()\n489\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n490\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n491\t                \"resolved\",\n492\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n493\t            )\n494\t\n495\t            return {\n496\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n497\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n498\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n499\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n500\t            }\n501\t\n502\t        try:\n503\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n504\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n505\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n506\t            else:\n507\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n508\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n509\t        except Exception:\n510\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n511\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n512\t            raise\n513\t\n514\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n515\t        return result\n516\t\n517\t    async def _persist_timings(\n518\t        self,\n519\t        intent_id: str,\n520\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n521\t    ) -> None:\n522\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n523\t        try:\n524\t            store = await self._get_store()\n525\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n526\t        except Exception as e:\n527\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n528\t\n529\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n530\t        self,\n531\t Args:\n538\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n539\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n540\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n541\t\n542\t        Returns:\n543\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n544\t        \"\"\"\n545\t        classification = {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n550\t        )\n551\t\n552\t        try:\n553\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n554\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n555\t                classification.intent_type,\n556\t                classification.lookup_kind\n557\t            )\n558\t\n559\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n560\t            from ..registry import get_project\n561\t            repo_path = None\n562\t            ssh_target = None\n563\t            host_alias = None\n564\t            registry = get_registry()\n565\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n566\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n567\t                if location = entry.display_path\n572\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n573\t                else:\n574\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n575\t\n576\t            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n577\t            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n578\t            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n579\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n580\t            project_cfg = (\n581\t                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n582\t                if classification.project_slug else None\n583\t            )\n584\t\n585\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n586\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n587\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n588\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n589\t                context=FetchContext(\n590\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n591\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n592\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n593\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n594\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n595\t app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n598\t                ),\n599\t            )\n600\t\n601\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first timings.clock()\n605\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n606\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n607\t            completed_sources:           # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n612\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n613\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n614\t\n615\t                # Increment completed count\n616\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n617\t\n618\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n619\t                  })\n652\t\n653\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n654\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n655\t                await }\n679\t\n680\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n681\t            required_sources_failed = [\n682\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n683\t                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n684\t                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n685\t            ]\n686\t\n687\t            if \"\"\"\n883\t        # Map enum values by string\n884\t        type_map = {\n885\t            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n886\t            IntentType.ACTION: intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n        self.parse_error = parse_error\n        self.raw_output = raw_output\n        self.retry_count = retry_count\n        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n
